description: Young Boys Inc. was a major African American drug organization based in Detroit, Michigan, founded in 1976 by Dwayne Davis, Bernard Boykins, Reginald Chestnut, and Charles Lindsey. It pioneered inner-city street-corner heroin sales using minors as couriers to avoid severe penalties. The group expanded to other cities like Boston and controlled up to 80% of Detroit's heroin trade until its leadership was indicted and convicted in 1982.

country_links.evidence_quote: After the split, Davis sent one of his top lieutenants to Boston to expand his operation. After about a year, the crew, along with new members from Boston, took over most of that city's heroin trade.
country_links.context: The organization operated in Detroit, Michigan, and expanded to Boston, Massachusetts, taking over the heroin trade in both cities.
